note: CLEAN PARETO WIN. Tile-size re-tuning on the rotated binary winner. Recall IDENTICAL across all tiles (exact-equivalent reordering) and single-query latency is tile-independent, so any QPS gain costs nothing. tile=8 = 921.6 QPS vs the previously-defaulted tile=16 = 857.4 -> +7.5% QPS, zero recall/latency cost. tile=16 is a REPRODUCIBLE local dip (CV<0.2%, confirmed reps=8) - a microarch cache resonance; tile=8 best, tile=32 (907) also beats it. New project-best high-recall throughput point: 0.9968 @ 922 (beats 030's tile=16 0.996 @ 845). Lesson: the default tile (16) was accidentally on a bad spot; the engine had ~7-9% free QPS the whole time.
